text: EPA — eicosapentaenoic acid — is the omega-3 most associated with mood regulation rather than structural brain architecture. Where DHA embeds itself in neuronal membranes, EPA works primarily through anti-inflammatory pathways. Chronic neuroinflammation, now linked to depression, anxiety, and cognitive fog, involves pro-inflammatory cytokines that disrupt neurotransmitter synthesis and impair the hypothalamic-pituitary axis. EPA's role is to compete with arachidonic acid for the enzymes that produce these cytokines, effectively dampening the inflammatory cascade. A 2022 meta-analysis in Translational Psychiatry confirmed that EPA-dominant formulas outperform DHA-dominant ones in clinical trials targeting depressive symptoms.
question: According to the passage, how does EPA reduce neuroinflammation?

options.text: By embedding in neuronal membranes to improve signal transmission
By stimulating BDNF production in the hippocampus
By competing with arachidonic acid for enzymes that produce pro-inflammatory cytokines
By increasing glucose uptake in the prefrontal cortex
